title: Manifold Valley & Market Drayton (Fair Oak, Broughton Hall)

main_route.excerpt: We reached the Manifold Valley & rented bikes & had a 3 hour bike ride with a picnic lunch.

main_route.normalized_name: Fair Oak Farm Campsite, near Loggerheads, Shropshire, UK
main_route.excerpt: We are camped in a lovely sheltered field near Broughton Hall, a Franciscan convent which is about to be sold. Fair Oak *** campsite
Stopping at the Wolverhampton camping shop on the way home, Peter bought a blow-up foot ball + Eve bought her long-awaited clothes-line pole.
Stopping at the Wolverhampton camping shop on the way home

excursions.label: Sunday church and country walk near Broughton Hall

stops.original_wording: old church which, they said, had been the chapel of Broughton Hall
stops.excerpt: a prayer book Communion at 11 in the old church which, they said, had been the chapel of Broughton Hall. It was very, very friendly.

unresolved.issue: Person named Charlotte sits with Simon and Eve at church - not in the known family list; possibly a friend or relative joining the trip.

summary: A short May Bank Holiday weekend trip in 1993 from Birmingham into Staffordshire and Shropshire. The family visited the Manifold Valley for a 3-hour bike ride on Saturday before camping at the Fair Oak campsite near Broughton Hall (a Franciscan convent) near Loggerheads and Market Drayton. On Sunday they attended a prayer-book Communion service in the old Broughton Hall chapel and walked 4.5 miles through the countryside before stopping at Wolverhampton on the way home.
extraction_notes: The diary describes the campsite on Saturday (A_0090_R) as 'near Broughton Hall' at 'Fair Oak campsite', and on Sunday (A_0091_L) as 'east of Market Drayton, near a village called Loggerheads'. These descriptions are consistent with the same location — Broughton Hall is near Loggerheads, a village on the A53 east of Market Drayton — so only one overnight_base is recorded. The Manifold Valley visit was a daytime stop en route on Saturday before arriving at camp. A 'Charlotte' appears once on A_0091_L seated with Simon and Eve at church; she is not in the known family list and has been flagged as unresolved. Year 1993 is inferred from date_guess in the bundle metadata; the diary pages give 'Saturday 8th May' (confirmed 8 May 1993 was a Saturday) and 'Sunday May 9th'.
